draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, conversational voice over corporate formality in an AI workspace interface
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality: friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual tone
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward approachable, human language and warm interactions in the workspace
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers polished, authoritative, or neutral professional communication style

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, conversational voice over corporate formality in an AI workspace interface
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward approachable, human language and warm interactions in the workspace

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer contained navigation paired with modular content areas over horizontal tab navigation
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore vertical nav hierarchies, card-based module systems, left-anchor navigation patterns for workspace tools
facade.rejectImplies: Test horizontal tab systems, full-width content layouts, top-anchor or floating navigation models

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may prefer ambient, organic light sources over clinical brightness to create psychological comfort during deep focus work.
facade.label: Warm overhead lighting
facade.acceptImplies: Prefer human-scaled, lived-in atmosphere; likely lean toward warm color palettes, soft shadows, and spaces that feel inhabited rather than sterile.
facade.rejectImplies: Prefer clarity and clinical precision; likely lean toward cool tones, high contrast, and environments that feel neutral or optimized for pure function.

facade.hypothesis: Users perceive AI responsiveness through residual motion cues rather than instant state changes
facade.label: Cursor trails fade
facade.acceptImplies: Motion residue and ghosting builds perceived fluidity and intelligence in the workspace; micro-animations should linger
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer crisp, instantaneous feedback with no motion artifacts; unnecessary animation reads as lag or distraction

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high contrast interfaces where dark backgrounds reduce cognitive load while neon or saturated colors highlight interactive elements
facade.label: Dark canvas with bright accents
facade.acceptImplies: Explore moody, high-contrast color palettes; prioritize dark mode; use vibrant accent colors for CTAs and key UI elements
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward lighter, more neutral palettes; test softer contrast ratios; consider pastel or muted accent colors instead

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users working in AI tools need prominent metric displays over dense data tables
facade.axisTargeted: charts vs text — whether key metrics should be large, scannable numbers or embedded in narrative prose
facade.label: Dashboard with big numbers
facade.acceptImplies: User wants quick visual scanning of KPIs; prefers metric-first design; likely favors card layouts, minimal labels, high contrast numbers
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers context-rich displays; wants numbers embedded in sentences or stories; likely favors prose, captions, or tables that show relationships over isolated metrics

facade.hypothesis: Users want personality and humor in AI workspace interactions, not just friendliness but also wit and lightness
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — serious vs playful spectrum
facade.label: Playful tone
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should use humor, witty copy, and unexpected delight to reduce cognitive load and build user confidence
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer straightforward, earnest communication without humor; playfulness feels unprofessional or distracting in work contexts

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
